subject: Economics
source: Liberia Ministry of Education - National Curriculum for Senior Secondary Schools

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to explain the meaning and fundamental concepts of Economics.
Learners are able to distinguish the branches of Economics and analyze basic economic problems.
objectives: Define and explain the origin of Economics
List and discuss the fundamental concepts in Economics
Relate the basic concepts of Economics to day-to-day experiences
Identify and discuss the basic problems in Economics
Categorize and explain the branches and types of Economic Systems
content: What is Economics — Definitions and origin of Economics
Fundamental Concepts of Economics: Scarcity, Choice, Scale of preference, Opportunity cost, Wants and Needs
Basic Economic Problems: What to produce, How to produce, For whom to produce
Branches of Economics — Micro and Macro Economics
Economic Systems: Capitalism/Free Enterprise, Socialism, Mixed Economy
activities: Give the origin and definitions of Economics
Exploration of basic Economics concepts — scarcity, choice, scale of preference, needs and wants and opportunity cost
Assign learners to research basic problems in Economics and types of Economic systems
Ask learners to debate the advantages and disadvantages of the different types of Economic systems
Discuss which system of Economics is practiced in Liberia
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

topics.topic: The Concepts of Demand and Supply
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to analyze the behaviors of consumers (demand side) and producers (supply side) in the market place and make rational decisions that relate to their consumption and production of goods and services.
objectives: Explain the meaning of demand and supply
Draw the demand and supply schedules
Derive the market demand and supply curves and establish the equilibrium or market price of a community
Identify and explain the factors affecting demand and supply of a commodity
Demonstrate the movement and shift of demand and supply curves
List and discuss the types of demand and supply for a commodity
content: Definitions: Demand and Supply laws
Demand and Supply schedules
Kinds of demand and supply
Relationship between supply and demand
Different graphs of demand and supply curves
Determination of equilibrium price and quantity
Determination of market clearing price, Shortages and Surplus
Movements and shift of demand and supply
Exceptional Demand Curve
Price ceiling and price floor
activities: Lead learners to discuss the laws of demand and supply and how they can apply the concept to consumption and production of goods and services
Group learners to analyze supply and demand based on factors and types
Assign learners to derive hypothetical supply and demand schedules to determine equilibrium price and quantity, changes in price at each level of demand and supply
Group learners to discuss the exceptional demand curves and give reasons for the abnormal behavior of the demand curve
Ask learners to discuss the concept of price floor and price ceiling and demonstrate its effects in the market
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to describe production and its types and the roles of each factor of production, examine the impact technological change can have in the production of goods and services and economic growth.
objectives: Define Production and its types
Describe the factors of production
Derive the production possibility curve
Explain the relationship between technology change and economic growth
Discuss division of labor, the concept of specialization and the law of diminishing returns
content: Definition and types of production
Factors of production: Land, Labor, Capital, Entrepreneurship and Technology
Factor remunerations: Land — Rent, Labor — Wages/Salary, Capital — Interest, Entrepreneurship — Profit
Production Possibility Curve/Frontier
Concept of division of labor, specialization and law of diminishing returns
activities: Lead discussion to brainstorm on the meaning of production and its types
Facilitate discussion to identify the factors of production and their importance and corresponding remunerations
Ask learners to research production, specialization and the law of diminishing returns

Assign learners to derive the production possibility curve of a hypothetical firm producing two goods to determine: Attainable region, Unattainable region, Full employment, Underutilization of resources
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to analyze and appreciate the concept of consumer behavior and demonstrate how utility works according to the laws of supply and demand.
Learners are able to demonstrate the ability to explain the indifference curve, budget line and consumer surplus.
objectives: Explain the Concepts of Utility
Discuss the Laws of Diminishing Marginal Utility (DMU)
Demonstrate the relationship between Total Utility and Marginal Utility
Cite examples to demonstrate the Marginal Utility Theory
Draw and discuss the graphs of indifference curve and consumer budget line
Illustrate the concept of consumer surplus
content: The concept of utility: Total and marginal utility, Utility maximization, The laws of diminishing utility
The relationship between Total and Marginal Utility
Application of Marginal Utility Theory
Indifference curve and marginal rate of substitution
Characteristics of the indifference curve and budget line
The concept of consumer surplus
activities: Lead learners to discuss definition and concept of utility, utility maximization, total and marginal utility
Form groups to discuss the importance of consumer behavior relative to the consumer budget line and the indifference curve
Assign learners to use the hypothetical firm utility schedule to demonstrate the concept of total and marginal utility, consumer budget line and consumer surplus
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate the skills of collecting, interpreting and analyzing economic data through percentage, ratios, tables, charts and graphical presentations.
Learners are able to interpret social data.
objectives: Define basic Economic tools and state their use and importance
Construct a Frequency Distribution Table
Measurement of Central Tendency — Mean, Median and Mode of any given set of data
content: Definition of basic economics tools: Tables, charts and graphs
Frequency distribution: Line graph, histograms, bar charts, pie-charts, component bar charts, pictograms
Measurement of Central Tendency: Calculation of Arithmetic Mean, Median and Mode
activities: Ask learners to determine the usages and importance of the basic tools of economic analysis
Community survey activity: Interview 100 teenage girls and boys about contraceptive use, present findings using pie chart/graph, and process information to show implication of data
Ask learners to present the ages of learners in class (data); calculate the mean, median and mode and show the implication of the data
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate understanding of how businesses are established and operated.
objectives: Define Sole-proprietorship, Partnership, Corporation, Joint-stock companies, Cooperatives, Statutory corporation, and Joint-ventures
State the main features of each type of business organization
Explain the advantages and disadvantages of Sole-proprietorship, Partnership and Corporation
Explain the sources of capital for each type of business organization
content: Definition of: Sole-proprietorship, Partnership, Corporation, Joint-stock companies, Cooperatives, Statutory corporation, and Joint-ventures
Main features of each business organization type
Advantages and Disadvantages of each type
Sources of funding for each type
Business enterprise policy on: Privatization, Commercialization, Indigenization, Nationalization
activities: Lead class discussion on the various types of businesses
Group learners to do presentations on the advantages and disadvantages of the types of business organizations
Assign tasks to individual learners to research the various sources of funding for sole-proprietorship, partnership and corporation
Ask learners to research the impact of Privatization, Commercialization, Indigenization and Nationalization policies on the Liberian economy
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate understanding of market forces of supply and demand.
Analyze price elasticity of demand and supply, and determine how changes in price will affect total revenue.
objectives: Identify and explain the interactions between supply and demand in determining the market price
Analyze the definitions of elasticity of demand and supply
Examine the price elasticity of demand and determine whether demand is elastic or inelastic
Calculate the price elasticity of demand using information from a demand curve
Analyze how changes in the price of a good affect total revenue and total expenditure
Compare cross-price elasticity of demand and income elasticity of demand
Discuss the price elasticity of supply
Calculate the price elasticity of supply using information from a supply curve
content: Determination of price by supply and demand: Equilibrium price and quantity, Shortage and Surplus, Changes in Equilibrium
Definition of: Price elasticity of demand and supply, Income elasticity, Inelasticity, Cross price-elasticity
Calculation of: Price elasticity of demand and supply, Income elasticity of demand and cross-elasticity
Calculation of: Price elasticity of demand and total revenue
Price elasticity of supply and total revenue
Determinants of price elasticity of demand and supply
activities: Group learners to identify and distinguish: Price elasticity of demand and supply, Income elasticity, Inelasticity, Cross price-elasticity
Pair learners to derive the formula for Elasticity and use available data to calculate Price elasticity of demand and supply, Income elasticity, Income Inelasticity, Cross price-elasticity
Pair learners to calculate the elasticity of a good based on its determinants
Ask individual learners to visit a local business center to investigate revenue (sales) and profit
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to describe the various types of markets and demonstrate how prices and total revenue are determined in those markets.
objectives: Explain the concept of market and distinguish it from the market place
Distinguish between perfect and imperfect markets
Compare and contrast perfect competitive and monopolistic markets
Draw graphs to illustrate price and quantity determination under Perfect competition, monopoly and oligopoly
content: Definitions and Features of market types: Perfect Market and Imperfect market, Monopolistic market, Oligopoly market
Advantages and Disadvantages of market types: Perfect competitive market, monopoly and oligopoly markets
Comparison and contrast of Perfect competitive market and Monopolistic market
Graphic illustrations of: Perfect competitive market and monopoly
activities: Group learners to research the features of various market structures
Pair learners into teams to discuss and illustrate advantages and disadvantages of the types of market structures
Assign individual learners tasks to compare and contrast perfect competitive market and monopolistic market
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

topics.topic: The Theory of Production and Distribution
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate skills in analyzing the processes of production and distribution of goods and services.
objectives: Explain the theory of production and distribution
Distinguish the various types of production
Determine the types of goods and wealth
Contrast firms, plant and industry
Distinguish fixed and variable inputs
Categorize the channels of distribution and their functions (producer, wholesaler, retailer)
Discuss the problems of Distribution and Marketing of Commodities in Liberia
content: The theory of Production and Distribution: Meaning of production, types of production, Classification of goods and description of the concept of wealth in Liberia
Differentiation of: firm, plant, industry, Fixed inputs, Variable inputs
Exploration of the distinguishing features of: Producer, Wholesaler, Retailer
Relating the concepts of producer, wholesaler, retailer and marketing practices in Liberia
activities: Lead discussions on the classification of goods and services, and understanding of the concepts of wealth in Liberian setting
Form groups to find distinguishing features of firm, plant and industry
Form pairs of learners to distinguish between Fixed inputs and Variable inputs
Individual learners to distinguish among Producer, wholesaler and retailer
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to analyze population dynamics and its influence on economic growth and development.
objectives: Define population and discuss the factors that influence population dynamics
Describe population size and its implication on the economy
State and critique the Malthusian theory of population
Analyze the links between population and the labor market
Explain population census, types of population census, importance and problems
content: Definition of: Population, Population census
Types of population census: De facto population census, De jure Population census
Importance of population census
Characteristics and problems associated with population census
Determinants of population size and growth: Birth rate, Death rate, Migration
Calculation of population density
Malthusian theory of population growth
Geographical distribution, age, sex and occupation distribution of population
Optimum population, under-population and over-population
activities: Lead discussion on the meaning of population and population census
Group learners to outline the types of population census according to the given features
Individual learners assign task to do head count of family members by gender and age distribution as well as determine dependence age and those of working age

Pair learners to respond to: What will happen to the labor force that is making significant contribution to national development, if they are struck with pandemic such as HIV/AIDS or other diseases — Give the social economic and development impact it has on the country, say Liberia
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to analyze the reasons business firms make decisions on production, profit and losses.
objectives: Demonstrate understanding of the meaning of costs and revenue
Distinguish between the various cost concepts
Analyze different costs and revenue curves
Calculate costs and revenue
content: Definitions of Cost and Revenue: Cost of Production and Revenue, Economist's and Accountant's view of cost, Explicit and Implicit cost
Cost Concepts: Fixed, Variable and Marginal cost; Average and Total cost; Short run and Long run cost
Derivation of cost and Revenue curves: Cost tables, Cost curves, Calculations of cost and Revenue
activities: Lead discussion to brainstorm on the definitions of cost and revenue
Group learners to demonstrate the cost of production and revenue concepts

Group learners to debate using the concepts of opportunity cost to discuss the effects of early marriage as against the benefit of Education relative to social and economic development of the individual — Cite regions or sections that are still practicing early marriage in Liberia (Girls vs Boys)
Group learners to formulate hypothetical firm cost schedule to calculate: Average cost, variable cost, marginal cost, total fixed cost, total variable cost, and total average cost
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

topics.topic: The Role of Agriculture in Economic Development
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to analyze and appreciate the role and importance of agriculture in social economic development of a nation.
objectives: Identify and discuss the major agriculture products in Liberia and West African Countries, and major export and import products/crops
Analyze the factors that hamper the agriculture sector in Liberia and elsewhere in West Africa
Explain the importance of agriculture products to economic development using Liberia as a case
Formulate recommendations that will enhance government agriculture policies
content: Agriculture products in Liberia/West Africa: Cash crops, Food crops, Livestock, Fisheries
Challenges of Agriculture production in Liberia/West Africa: Land, Subsidy, Storage, Education, Infrastructure (Roads), Technology
activities: Lead learners to brainstorm and discuss the importance of agriculture to national development — check for participation and inclusiveness
Ask learners to discuss the challenges faced in the Agriculture sector of Liberia and recommend how these challenges can be alleviated
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

topics.topic: Economic Development and Planning (The Liberian Economy)
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate the concept of economic development and appreciate the important role planning plays in the development of a nation.
objectives: Explain the concept of Economic Development and Planning
Distinguish between Economic growth and Economic Development
Explain the features of underdeveloped, developing and developed economy
Analyze the concept of Liberian Traditional Economy
Determine the factors which influence economic development in Liberia and West African Countries
content: Overview of the concepts of Economic Development and Planning; Economic Growth and Economic Development
Features of underdeveloped, developing and developed economy
Concept of Liberian Traditional Economy
Factors which influence economic development in Liberia and West African Countries
activities: Lead learners to discuss Economic Development and Planning; Economic Growth and Economic Development
Ask learners to research and chart the features of underdeveloped, developing and developed economy
Group learners to research and debate the Concept of Liberian Traditional Economy
Pair learners to research the factors which influence economic development in Liberia and West African Countries and propose recommendations
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

topics.topic: Industrialization of the Liberian Economy and Concepts of National Income
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to determine how decisions made by policy makers can influence economic development and growth of a country.
objectives: Analyze the concept of industrialization and discuss how it is related to Liberia's economic development
Analyze the process involved in industrialization
Determine measures of the country's economic activities
Outline the methods used to calculate economic activities in the economy
Discuss the limitations/pitfalls of calculating national income
Suggest policy options that could improve the economy of Liberia
content: Meaning of: Industrialization, Industrialization policies, Localization of industries
Role of Industrialization in economic development: Optimum Utilization of Scarce Resources, Provision of Machinery to increase Agriculture productivity
Measuring national income: GNP, NNP, GDP per capita
Uses of national income statistics
Circular flow diagram of income and expenditure
Methods of calculating national income
Advantages and limitations of GNP, Per Capita Income calculation
activities: Group learners to research and debate industrialization in the context of Liberia and other countries in West Africa
Organize a field trip with learners to an industrial site and allow learners to observe the processes of production and distribution and present report on their findings
Facilitate discussions to explore the meaning and importance of National Income
Pair learners to research and determine the methods and measures of calculation of National Income
Pair learners to research and determine the limitations of National Income
Group learners to identify major development projects financed by national income to compare government expenditure on each project and how these projects have impacted the lives of Liberians
Group assignment: Demonstrate linkages of the different actors in the economy — Circular Flow Diagram
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

topics.topic: Money, Banking and the Financial Institutions
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to determine the importance of money and the role banking institutions play in the economy.
objectives: Analyze and discuss the origin, meaning, functions and types of money
Catalogue and discuss the characteristics of money
Analyze the types and functions of banks and other financial institutions
Catalogue and discuss inflation, types, causes and how it can be controlled
Demonstrate the relations between inflation and unemployment
content: Money: Definition and origin (development of), Characteristics and functions, Value for money and price
Functions of Banks/Financial Institutions: Central Bank, Commercial Banks, Merchant Banks, Development Banks, Saving Banks, Insurance companies, Building societies
Definition of: Inflation, Unemployment
Types of inflation and unemployment: Demand pull, Cost push inflation, Structural unemployment, Cyclical unemployment, Frictional unemployment
activities: Lead learners to brainstorm and discuss the definition, origin, characteristics, and the functions of money
Group learners to research: Central Bank, Commercial Banks, Merchant Banks, Development Banks, Saving Banks, Insurance companies and Building societies
Organize learners into two groups to debate the merits and demerits of inflation and unemployment in the economy
Pair learners to research and discuss: Demand pull, Cost push inflation, Structural unemployment, Cyclical unemployment, Frictional unemployment
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

topics.topic: International Trade and Balance of Payments
learning_outcomes: Learners can utilize their skills and knowledge to analyze the effects of policies on international trade and its benefits to the economic development of the nation.
objectives: Discuss the term International Trade
Elaborate on acceptable reasons for trade between nations
Determine and discuss factors that are involved in international trade
Analyze the reasons for the variance in currencies among countries
Discuss the concept of Legal tender
content: Definition: International Trade, Domestic Trade, Regional Trade, Terms of Trade, Balance of Trade
Features/Reasons for International Trade
Differences between domestic and International trade
The law of comparative cost: Free Trade and protection (UNCTAD), Law of comparative and Absolute Advantage
Balance of Payments (BOP): The use of foreign currency, Disequilibrium/Adjustment, Correcting balance of payments, Devaluation and Depreciation of currency, Balance of Payment deficit and surplus
activities: Group learners to discuss: International Trade, Domestic Trade, Regional Trade, Terms of Trade, Balance of Trade

Pair learners to research: Importance of international trade in relationship to the development of the country, Advantages and disadvantages of international trade, Comparative and absolute advantage of international trade
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to explain government fiscal policy and appreciate how it is used to provide better services that improve the livelihood of the citizenry.
objectives: Determine what public finance is and how it is important to public expenditure
Explain government fiscal measures (taxation and expenditure)
Explain the role of taxation in the economy
Illustrate graphically the incidence of taxation
Catalogue and discuss the roles of major international economics organizations
content: Meaning of Public finance: Fiscal policy and its objectives, Sources of Government Revenue (Taxation), Economic Effect of taxation
Types of taxes: Direct and Indirect Taxes
Advantages and Disadvantages of direct and Indirect Taxes
Tax incidence, Elasticity of Demand/Supply and Taxation
System of taxation: Progressive tax, Proportional tax, Regressive tax
International Economic Organizations: IMF, IBRD, IFC, ADB, OPEC, ECA, UNCTAD, AfDB
activities: Group learners to discuss the meaning of public finance and its objectives

Assign small groups of learners to business institutions that generate revenues and are paying taxes; let learners examine the types and amount of taxes paid and revenue earned in a fiscal year and report to class

Pair learners to discuss: Elasticity of demand/supply and taxation, Indicate tax incidence (burden of tax), Tax systems — distinguish and analyze the effects of tax on a family income, business firm and government
Assign individual learners to develop a research paper on international economic organizations and indicate how Liberia has benefited from: IMF, IBRD, IFC, ADB, OPEC, ECA, UNCTAD, AfDB
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to appreciate the value of regional cooperation and trade.
objectives: Determine the rationale for Economic Integration in the sub-region
Discuss and analyze the concept of Economic integration and its impact on regional trade
Catalogue the advantages and disadvantages of Economic Integration
Discuss the stages of Economic Integration
Compare natural resources of Liberia to countries in the sub-region
content: Definition of Economic Integration and key concepts: Trade benefits, Employment, Political cooperation, Market Expansion, Technology sharing, Cross-border Investment flows
Stages of Economic Integration: Preferential trading area, Free trading area, Customs union, Common market, Economic and monetary union and fiscal policy harmonization
Advantages and Disadvantages of Economic Integration
Natural resources of Liberia/Sub-region
activities: Group learners to research and discuss Economics Integration concepts: Trade benefits, Employment, Political cooperation, Market expansion, Technology sharing, Investment flow — observe learner participation and interactions

Assign learners to research the stages of Economic Integration and determine whether it has impacted the economy of Liberia. Let learners analyze the advantages and disadvantages of regional trade and cooperation
topics.primary_text: Stanlake's Economics by S.J. Grant, 8th Edition
secondary_texts: Essential Economics for Senior Secondary Schools (Cole Esan Ande), 5th Edition, 2017
Comprehensive Economics for Senior Secondary School (Johnson U. Anyaele, 1997)
